name: Blue Cornmeal Chicken Wings
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Crushed Red Pepper Flakes, or to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large glass bowl, mix lime juice, vegetable oil, and crushed red pepper flakes.
2. Cut each chicken wing at joints to make 3 pieces; discard the tip. Remove excess skin.
3. Place wings in the oil mixture; stir to coat. Cover and refrigerate for at least 3 hours, stirring occasionally. Drain before using.
4. Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C).
5. Melt butter or margarine in a rectangular pan, 13x9x2 inches, in the oven until melted.
6. Mix remaining ingredients (cornmeal, flour, salt, cumin, and pepper) in a plastic bag or bowl.
7. Shake wings in cornmeal mixture to coat; place them in the pan.
8. Bake uncovered for 20 minutes, then turn. Continue baking until golden brown, for an additional 20-25 minutes.
9. Makes 20 appetizers. The heat level can be adjusted by adding more crushed red pepper flakes if desired.
